name: Simple Yogurt Bread
description: A quick and easy mini loaf of bread made with Greek yogurt and self-raising flour, perfect for air frying or oven baking. House calibration: sugar-substitutes → granulated Splenda (volume-matched); any light butter → real butter at matched calories. Imported via `batch ingest`.

steps.instructionText: Mix flour, yogurt, and salt in a bowl until it clumps together.
Using floured hands, shape the dough into a boule. It should not be sticky. Add more flour if necessary.
Air fry at 175°C for 15-20 minutes using the air fry mode. Alternatively, oven bake at 175°C for 35 minutes. If the center is not cooked, flip the bread and cook for an additional 5 minutes.

notes.text: The dough should clump together immediately and feel like stiff playdough — if it's wet and shaggy the yogurt was too thin (brand matters). Add the extra flour before shaping, not after, or the surface gets uneven.
Shape it into a tight boule before air frying: a loose, uneven mound will puff unevenly and the center stays raw while the outside firms. Tuck the seam underneath.
Air fry at 175C: don't go higher chasing speed — the crust sets and hardens before the center (only 120 g flour + 100 g yogurt) finishes cooking, leading to a gummy core.
Check doneness by tapping the bottom — it should sound hollow. The flip-and-5-min method is the right call if the center feels dense; internal temp target is ~93C (200F).
This bread has no fat and very little protein per slice from the yogurt alone — it stales quickly. Eat within a day or freeze slices individually.
